body.title: agent_datacenter: lock Claude mailbox naming convention (CC.0 + CC.N per-session)
body.result: constants.py in devices/claude/: GLOBAL_MAILBOX='CC.0', get_session_mailbox() reads CLAUDE_SESSION_ID env. docs/decisions/mailbox-naming.md written. All tests pass.
body.description:
Lock Claude mailbox naming before device code is written. All Claude device code imports these constants.

Decision confirmed by Akien 2026-04-27:
- CC.0 = global/broadcast Claude mailbox (legacy-compatible, always present)
- CC.{session_id} = per-session isolated mailbox for multi-CC scenarios
- Default delivery target = CC.0 unless caller specifies session ID
- Session ID sourced from CLAUDE_SESSION_ID env var when set
- Rationale: multi-CC scenarios exist (4-machine swarm); per-session avoids cross-talk; CC.0 is the fallback broadcast

Proposed shape:
- agent_datacenter/devices/claude/constants.py:
  GLOBAL_MAILBOX = 'CC.0'
  SESSION_MAILBOX_PREFIX = 'CC.'
  SESSION_ID_ENV_VAR = 'CLAUDE_SESSION_ID'
  def get_session_mailbox() -> str: reads env var, returns CC.{id} or CC.0 as fallback
- docs/decisions/mailbox-naming.md: decision narrative + rationale

Affected files:
- ~/dev/src/agent_datacenter/devices/claude/constants.py (new)
- ~/dev/src/agent_datacenter/docs/decisions/mailbox-naming.md (new)

Design rules:
- oop-first: get_session_mailbox() is a function in the module, not a global — testable in isolation
- docs-in-code: constants.py module docstring explains the naming scheme

Scope boundary:
IN: naming constants, helper function, decision doc
OUT: any IMAP mailbox creation code (T-adc-imap-mailbox-lifecycle, Phase 1)

Test plan:
- constants.py imports cleanly
- GLOBAL_MAILBOX == 'CC.0'
- get_session_mailbox() returns 'CC.0' when env var absent
- get_session_mailbox() returns 'CC.abc123' when CLAUDE_SESSION_ID='abc123'
